goal: Bin picking with random-pose cube detection: a Franka arm picks cubes tumbled into an unstructured open-top bin at random orientations. Before each pick cycle the robot queries overlap_sphere centered on the bin to enumerate candidate objects, selects the topmost item by bounding-box height, and uses define_grasp_pose to register a top-down approach. A grasp-pose sampler (setup_grasp_pose_sampler, antipodal mode) is pre-configured for potential SDG use. cuRobo executes collision-free plans to pick each cube and deposit it in a flat outfeed bin beside the robot. Exercises overlap_sphere for collision-scene candidate detection, define_grasp_pose for grasp YAML generation, setup_grasp_pose_sampler for SDG grasp library prep, and setup_pick_place_controller for motor execution.
thoughts:
Design rationale for CP-NEW-bin-picking-random-pose:

1) Distinctiveness: This canonical is the ONLY one in the new library to use overlap_sphere (scene-query candidate enumeration), define_grasp_pose (YAML grasp spec), and setup_grasp_pose_sampler (antipodal SDG prep) together. Heap-zone-unstack uses heap_zone + bounding_box but NOT the grasp-spec tools. Palletizer and kit-prep use a fixed source list. Here the source list is discovered at runtime via physics query.

2) Scene layout: Franka at [0, 0, 0.75] on a 0.75m table. Source bin at [0, 0.42, 0.80]: bin external [0.35, 0.35, 0.20], walls 10mm thick, interior 0.33x0.33x0.19m. Bin floor z = 0.80. 6 cubes (0.08m) tumbled into bin: expected random pile, top cube z ≈ 0.80 + 0.08*3 = 1.04 at most. Outfeed bin at [0, -0.48, 0.80]: same dimensions, placed behind robot for drop destination.

3) overlap_sphere query: center=[0, 0.42, 0.92] (bin interior mid-height), radius=0.22m (covers full bin interior volume). PhysX AABB overlaps return all rigid-body prim paths currently colliding with the query sphere. This is the runtime mechanism for finding unsorted cubes — no pre-built path list needed. Filter results to paths matching /World/Bin/Cube_* pattern to exclude bin walls.

4) define_grasp_pose: called once per cube path with approach_direction=[0, 0, -1] (top-down) and gripper_offset=[0, 0, 0.05] (5cm above object centroid for open-jaw approach). Writes workspace/grasp_poses/Cube_N.isaac_grasp YAML. This is a pre-run setup step; the grasp spec is referenced by the pick-place controller in production use.

5) setup_grasp_pose_sampler: configured for the source-bin prim (/World/SourceBin), n_samples=60, sampling_mode='antipodal'. This attaches a marker attribute set to the bin prim for downstream SDG Replicator use. Does not affect the current pick-place execution path — it is a preparatory step.

6) Height-ranked pick selection: get_bounding_box called for each candidate path returned by overlap_sphere. Sort by bbox max_z descending. Pass sorted list as source_paths to setup_pick_place_controller with curobo. This is the correct runtime pattern per heap-zone-unstack precedent (A8).

7) drop_targets: all 6 cubes map to [0, -0.48, 0.89] — interior floor of outfeed bin (z = 0.80 bin_pos + 0.08 wall_height + 0.005 = 0.885 ≈ 0.89 with cube half-size buffer).

8) Physics: CPU dynamics (MBP broadphase) for deterministic bin contacts. sleepThreshold=0.0 on cubes. rubber material reduces inter-cube sliding. Settle time: 3s before first overlap_sphere call so cubes are at rest in the bin.

9) Franka reach check: source bin at [0, +0.42], outfeed bin at [0, -0.48]. Max horizontal reach to far outfeed bin edge corner: sqrt(0.48^2 + 0.175^2) ≈ 0.51m. Well inside Franka 0.855m reach. Transit height = 1.18m clears all bin walls.
code:
# ── Scene skeleton ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
create_prim(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", prim_type="DomeLight")
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", attr_name="inputs:intensity", value=1000.0)
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Ground", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, -0.5], scale=[20, 20, 1])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Ground", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Cell", prim_type="Xform")

# ── Robot table platform ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Table", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, 0.375], scale=[0.6, 0.6, 0.375])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Table", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

# ── Physics: CPU dynamics for deterministic bin contacts ──────────────────────
set_physics_scene_config(config={"enable_gpu_dynamics": False, "broadphase_type": "MBP"})

# ── Franka arm ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Mounted on 0.75m table. Source bin at y=+0.42, outfeed bin at y=-0.48.
# Max horizontal reach to far outfeed corner ~0.51m — inside 0.855m Franka reach.
robot_wizard(
    robot_name="franka",
    dest_path="/World/Franka",
    position=[0, 0, 0.75],
    orientation=[1, 0, 0, 0],
)

# ── Source bin: open-top container for tumbled cubes ─────────────────────────
# External 0.35x0.35x0.20m. Interior 0.33x0.33x0.19m. Floor at z=0.80.
# Walls 10mm thick (PhysX reliable minimum for contained objects).
create_bin(
    prim_path="/World/SourceBin",
    size=[0.35, 0.35, 0.20],
    position=[0, 0.42, 0.80],
    wall_thickness=0.01,
)

# ── Outfeed bin: drop destination ────────────────────────────────────────────
# Same geometry, placed behind robot (y=-0.48). Cubes drop to interior floor.
create_bin(
    prim_path="/World/OutfeedBin",
    size=[0.35, 0.35, 0.20],
    position=[0, -0.48, 0.80],
    wall_thickness=0.01,
)

# ── 6 tumbled cubes (0.08m) in source bin ────────────────────────────────────
# Spawn at staggered XY positions within bin interior; random orientations
# emerge when PhysX settles them under gravity. Each cube is 0.08m — three
# layers fit inside the 0.19m interior height before stacking overflows.
cube_paths = [f"/World/SourceBin/Cube_{i+1}" for i in range(6)]
cube_spawn_offsets = [
    [-0.08, -0.08, 0.15], [ 0.08, -0.08, 0.17],
    [-0.08,  0.08, 0.22], [ 0.08,  0.08, 0.24],
    [ 0.00, -0.04, 0.29], [ 0.00,  0.04, 0.31],
]

for i, (path, offset) in enumerate(zip(cube_paths, cube_spawn_offsets)):
    # World position: bin base [0, 0.42, 0.80] + local offset
    world_pos = [0 + offset[0], 0.42 + offset[1], 0.80 + offset[2]]
    create_prim(prim_path=path, prim_type="Cube", position=world_pos, size=0.08)
    for api in ("PhysicsRigidBodyAPI", "PhysicsCollisionAPI", "PhysicsMassAPI", "PhysxRigidBodyAPI"):
        apply_api_schema(prim_path=path, schema_name=api)

bulk_set_attribute(
    prim_paths=cube_paths,
    attr="physxRigidBody:sleepThreshold",
    value=0.0,
)
for path in cube_paths:
    apply_physics_material(prim_path=path, material_name="rubber")

# ── setup_grasp_pose_sampler: pre-configure antipodal grasp SDG library ───────
# Attaches grasp:* attributes to SourceBin for downstream Replicator SDG use.
# Does NOT affect live pick-place execution — preparatory step only.
setup_grasp_pose_sampler(
    sampler_path="/World/GraspSampler",
    target_path="/World/SourceBin",
    n_samples=60,
    sampling_mode="antipodal",
)

# ── Arrival confirmation sensor in outfeed bin ────────────────────────────────
add_proximity_sensor(
    sensor_path="/World/ArrivalSensor",
    position=[0, -0.48, 0.895],
    size=[0.28, 0.28, 0.08],
)

# ── Runtime: overlap_sphere candidate discovery + height-ranked pick ──────────
# Step 1: query PhysX AABB overlaps within source bin volume.
# Sphere center at bin interior mid-height; radius covers full interior.
_candidates_raw = overlap_sphere(
    center=[0, 0.42, 0.92],
    radius=0.22,
)
# Filter to cube paths (exclude bin wall prims)
_cube_set = set(cube_paths)
_candidates = [
    p for p in (_candidates_raw.get("prim_paths") or [])
    if p in _cube_set
]
# Fallback to full list if query returns empty (physics not yet stepped)
if not _candidates:
    _candidates = cube_paths

# Step 2: define top-down grasp spec for each candidate
for _path in _candidates:
    define_grasp_pose(
        robot_path="/World/Franka",
        object_path=_path,
        approach_direction=[0, 0, -1],
        gripper_offset=[0, 0, 0.05],
    )

# Step 3: height-rank candidates using bounding box
_bboxes = []
for _path in _candidates:
    _bb = get_bounding_box(prim_path=_path)
    _max_z = _bb.get("max", [0, 0, 0])[2] if isinstance(_bb, dict) else 0.0
    _bboxes.append((_path, _max_z))
_bboxes.sort(key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)
sorted_cube_paths = [p for p, _ in _bboxes]

# Step 4: all cubes drop to outfeed bin interior centre at z=0.89
# (bin_base_z=0.80 + floor_t=0.01 + cube_half=0.04 + small_gap=0.04 = 0.89)
drop_targets = {p: [0, -0.48, 0.89] for p in sorted_cube_paths}

# ── cuRobo pick-place controller ──────────────────────────────────────────────
setup_pick_place_controller(
    robot_path="/World/Franka",
    target_source="curobo",
    source_paths=sorted_cube_paths,
    destination_path="/World/OutfeedBin",
    drop_targets=drop_targets,
    planning_obstacles=[
        "/World/Table",
        "/World/SourceBin",
        "/World/OutfeedBin",
    ],
    sensor_path="/World/ArrivalSensor",
)
failure_modes: overlap_sphere returns empty list before physics step — the overlap_sphere query fires at scene-build time before cubes have settled. The code falls back to the static cube_paths list; if overlap_sphere returns paths to bin wall prims (floor/walls), the filter `if p in _cube_set` removes them. If cubes are not yet rigid-body-initialized, the fallback ensures no pick is skipped.

define_grasp_pose YAML write fails — workspace/grasp_poses/ may not exist if the working directory differs from project root. The _gen_define_grasp_pose handler calls os.makedirs(..., exist_ok=True), so the directory is created automatically. If a filesystem permission error occurs, the tool prints an error and pick execution continues (grasp YAML is advisory, not required for curobo execution).

Cube resting against bin wall occludes grasp — cubes in contact with a wall may be inaccessible for top-down approach. cuRobo collision checking with SourceBin in planning_obstacles will reject paths that clip the wall. Mitigation: increase gripper_offset z from 0.05 to 0.08m; alternatively, add a pre-grasp lateral nudge by reducing approach height clearance check.

Height-ranked sort stale — get_bounding_box is called after overlap_sphere; if cubes are still settling at query time, bbox_max_z may reflect mid-fall positions. sleepThreshold=0.0 and 3s settle time in the function-gate harness mitigate this; in live use, add a sim.step(n=60) before the sort.

OutfeedBin wall collision on drop — drop target [0, -0.48, 0.89] is 0.04m above the bin interior floor. If cuRobo approaches from the side (non-top-down), EE may clip the 0.20m tall bin wall. Planning_obstacles includes '/World/OutfeedBin' so cuRobo avoids the walls; EE approaches from above at transit height 1.18m and descends vertically.
code_template:
# ── Scene skeleton ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
create_prim(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", prim_type="DomeLight")
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", attr_name="inputs:intensity", value=1000.0)
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Ground", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, -0.5], scale=[20, 20, 1])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Ground", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Cell", prim_type="Xform")
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Table", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, 0.375], scale=[0.6, 0.6, 0.375])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Table", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

set_physics_scene_config(config={"enable_gpu_dynamics": False, "broadphase_type": "MBP"})

robot_wizard(
    robot_name={{primary_robot.class}},
    dest_path={{primary_robot.path}},
    position={{primary_robot.position}},
    orientation={{primary_robot.orientation}},
)

create_bin(
    prim_path={{source_bin.path}},
    size={{source_bin.size}},
    position={{source_bin.position}},
    wall_thickness={{source_bin.wall_thickness}},
)

create_bin(
    prim_path={{destination_bin.path}},
    size={{destination_bin.size}},
    position={{destination_bin.position}},
    wall_thickness={{destination_bin.wall_thickness}},
)

cube_paths = [{{workpieces[0].path}}, {{workpieces[1].path}}, {{workpieces[2].path}}, {{workpieces[3].path}}, {{workpieces[4].path}}, {{workpieces[5].path}}]

# Spawn each cube at bin_base + spawn_offset
create_prim(prim_path={{workpieces[0].path}}, prim_type="Cube", position=[0+{{workpieces[0].spawn_offset}}[0], 0.42+{{workpieces[0].spawn_offset}}[1], 0.80+{{workpieces[0].spawn_offset}}[2]], size=0.08)
for api in ("PhysicsRigidBodyAPI", "PhysicsCollisionAPI", "PhysicsMassAPI", "PhysxRigidBodyAPI"):
    apply_api_schema(prim_path={{workpieces[0].path}}, schema_name=api)

bulk_set_attribute(
    prim_paths=cube_paths,
    attr="physxRigidBody:sleepThreshold",
    value=0.0,
)
for path in cube_paths:
    apply_physics_material(prim_path=path, material_name="rubber")

setup_grasp_pose_sampler(
    sampler_path="/World/GraspSampler",
    target_path={{source_bin.path}},
    n_samples=60,
    sampling_mode="antipodal",
)

add_proximity_sensor(
    sensor_path="/World/ArrivalSensor",
    position=[0, -0.48, 0.895],
    size=[0.28, 0.28, 0.08],
)

_candidates_raw = overlap_sphere(
    center=[0, 0.42, 0.92],
    radius=0.22,
)
_cube_set = set(cube_paths)
_candidates = [p for p in (_candidates_raw.get("prim_paths") or []) if p in _cube_set]
if not _candidates:
    _candidates = cube_paths

for _path in _candidates:
    define_grasp_pose(
        robot_path={{primary_robot.path}},
        object_path=_path,
        approach_direction=[0, 0, -1],
        gripper_offset=[0, 0, 0.05],
    )

_bboxes = []
for _path in _candidates:
    _bb = get_bounding_box(prim_path=_path)
    _max_z = _bb.get("max", [0, 0, 0])[2] if isinstance(_bb, dict) else 0.0
    _bboxes.append((_path, _max_z))
_bboxes.sort(key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)
sorted_cube_paths = [p for p, _ in _bboxes]

drop_targets = {p: [0, -0.48, 0.89] for p in sorted_cube_paths}

setup_pick_place_controller(
    robot_path={{primary_robot.path}},
    target_source="curobo",
    source_paths=sorted_cube_paths,
    destination_path={{destination_bin.path}},
    drop_targets=drop_targets,
    planning_obstacles=["/World/Table", {{source_bin.path}}, {{destination_bin.path}}],
    sensor_path="/World/ArrivalSensor",
)
